danger_signals.visual_form: Military architecture, tank turrets with missile batteries, Nazi base structures
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile territory; enemy faction stronghold requiring combat or stealth

danger_signals.visual_form: Underground corridors requiring grenades to pass, one-way walls, sealed passages
danger_signals.meaning: High-security/hazardous zones; progression blocked by resource scarcity (grenade count)

danger_signals.visual_form: First-person 3D shooting mode with tank and missile battery encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Combat-focused danger state; ranged threat environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Opening cinematic: helicopter transporting biological weapon vial 'Cassandra G' destroyed near Statue of Liberty
danger_signals.meaning: Catastrophic threat level; world-state indicates biological WMD loose in environment

safety_signals.visual_form: Interior spaces where Cherry Grace and Eve Christy appear (Acts 2 and 4-5)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for mission briefings and character interactions; temporary respite from combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Automatic elimination sequences (Hitler/Smirk brain room suggests side-view kill animation)
safety_signals.meaning: Mission objective achieved; controlled threat neutralization

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nazi/militaristic insignia (censored in US as 'Drek' but retained visual iconography)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Tank turrets with missile batteries; military base architecture; Adolf Hitler/Smirk as leader figure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military bases (specifically noted: Nazi Base), fortified underground facilities, Cold War locations (East Berlin, Antarctica)
Helicopter transport, international mission locations (Berlin, Athens, Rio, Alexander Island), hotel rendezvous points

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Female character models in hotel scenes; intelligence operative role
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hotel interiors; meeting locations between acts

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Biological WMD threat (Cassandra G vial missing)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opening helicopter destruction; vial nowhere to be found in wreckage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Triggers Golgo 13 assassination mission against Drek leader; mission chain across multiple geographic acts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level locations: East Berlin, Athens, Rio de Janeiro, Alexander Island (Antarctica); Nazi/Drek military infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple acts navigating international locations; infiltration and assassination across enemy territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Underground basements with restricted passages; grenade-locked corridors; missile battery placements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Resource-gated progression (grenades required); stealth/combat alternatives through 3D maze segments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hitler/Smirk brain room (unused content suggests side-view kill animation); 'Next Episode' screen appears after level completion in JP version
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Automatic target elimination; narrative progression to next act

environmental_storytelling.example: Acts advance through real Cold War locations (East Berlin, Athens, Rio, Antarctica) establishing geopolitical context for assassination mission
Nazi/Drek military bases use militaristic architecture with tank turrets and missile batteries; distinct visual language from civilian hotel interiors
Game provides only 3 grenades but level design requires 4 to access Act 3 basement, suggesting supply chain vulnerability or player limitation
Missing 'Cassandra G' vial in helicopter wreckage establishes threat environment justifying entire mission structure
Hotel rendezvous sequences (Cherry Grace, Eve Christy) appear between acts; visual transition from combat to dialogue establishes temporary narrative/safety breaks

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Traverses environments, collects grenades, navigates one-way walls; can jump to higher ledges
Encounters tank turrets with missile batteries; combat-focused threat response
Automatic elimination sequence; mission-objective completion behavior
Stationary or mobile turret fire; range-based threat indicator
Brain room location suggests possible side-view platformer fight (unused); typically auto-eliminated in sniper mode

notes: Golgo 13 is a hybrid action game combining side-view platforming, 3D maze navigation, and sniper-mode encounters. World-state communicates primarily through geographic Cold War locations and Nazi/Drek military architecture. The missing biological weapon (Cassandra G) serves as the central plot driver establishing danger context. US censorship renamed 'Nazi' to 'Drek' and 'hitman' to 'sniper,' but visual architecture retained military iconography. Hotel rendezvous sequences break mission tension and signal safe zones. Resource gating (grenade scarcity) and one-way level design reinforce infiltration/escape narrative. Unused Hitler brain room suggests alternative boss confrontation design not fully implemented.
